task.name: Create shared PageHeader component
task.action:
Create `src/components/shared/PageHeader.tsx` with the following: 1. Define `PageHeaderProps` interface with `title` (string, required) and `actions` (React.ReactNode, optional). 2. Export named component `PageHeader`. 3. Render structure: ```tsx

{title}

{actions &&
{actions}
}
``` 4. Use named export (not default export), consistent with other shared components.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build` to confirm the new file compiles without errors.

task.name: Refactor Dashboard page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`: 1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the wrapper div (lines 27-37) that contains both the h1 and the mobile logout button with: ```tsx ``` 3. KEEP the `handleLogout`, `signOut`, `useRouter`, and `Icons` imports for now -- they will be removed in Phase 2 task "Remove dashboard logout button". For this task, only move the logout button out of the header area. Actually, since the h1 wrapper div ALSO contains the logout button, and we are replacing the whole div with PageHeader, the logout button disappears. Therefore: - The `handleLogout` function, `signOut` import, `useRouter` import, and `Icons` import become unused. - Remove all of them in this task. - Remove the `"use client"` directive only if the page no longer has any client-side hooks. Check: `React` import is still used (JSX). No hooks remain. However, child components may need client context. Keep `"use client"` to be safe since the page imports client components. 4. Final dashboard page should look like: ```tsx "use client" import React from 'react' import NetWorthSection from '@/components/dashboard/NetWorthSection' import RecentTransactions from '@/components/dashboard/RecentTransactions' import { AccountsSummary } from '@/components/dashboard/AccountsSummary' import { BudgetStatus } from '@/components/shared/BudgetStatus' import { PageHeader } from '@/components/shared/PageHeader' const Dashboard = () => { return (
...rest unchanged...
) } ```
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Run `npx vitest run` to check for broken tests.

task.name: Refactor Accounts page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/accounts/page.tsx`: 1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Find the header block (lines ~79-107): ```

Accounts

...desktop button... ...CreateAccountSheet... ...mobile button... ...CreateAccountDrawer...
``` 3. Replace with: ```tsx } /> ``` 4. Move the CreateAccountSheet and CreateAccountDrawer components OUTSIDE of PageHeader (they are portals, they can be placed anywhere in the JSX tree). They may already be elsewhere -- check and ensure they are not inside the actions prop.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Cards page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/cards/page.tsx`:
1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the header block (lines ~151-178) with `<PageHeader title="Cards" actions={...}>`. 3. Pass the desktop + mobile "Add credit card" buttons as `actions`. 4. Move CreateCardSheet and CreateCardDrawer outside the actions prop. 5. Follow the same pattern as the Accounts refactor.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Expenses page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/expenses/page.tsx`:
1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the header block (lines ~78-108) with `<PageHeader title="Expenses" actions={...}>`. 3. Pass the desktop + mobile "Add expense" buttons as `actions`. 4. Move CreateExpenseTransactionSheet and CreateExpenseTransactionDrawer outside the actions prop.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Income page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/income/page.tsx`:
1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the header block (lines ~125-155) with `<PageHeader title="Income" actions={...}>`. 3. Pass the desktop + mobile "Add income category" buttons as `actions`. 4. Move CreateIncomeTypeSheet and CreateIncomeTypeDrawer outside the actions prop.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Budgets page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/budgets/page.tsx`:
1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the header block (lines ~78-108) with `<PageHeader title="Budgets" actions={...}>`. 3. Pass the desktop + mobile "Add budget" buttons as `actions`. 4. Move CreateExpenseTypeSheet and CreateExpenseTypeDrawer outside the actions prop.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Transfers page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/transfers/page.tsx`: 1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the standalone h1 (line ~67): ```tsx

Transfers

``` with: ```tsx ``` 3. No actions -- Transfers page has no create button in the header.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Transactions page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/transactions/page.tsx`: 1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the h1 (lines ~8-10): ```tsx

Transactions

``` with: ```tsx
``` Note: The `mb-6` was on the h1 itself. Move it to a wrapper div around PageHeader to preserve spacing. 3. No actions.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor Reports page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/reports/page.tsx`: 1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the header block (lines ~63-65): ```tsx

Reports

``` with: ```tsx ``` 3. No actions.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Refactor More page to use PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/app/more/page.tsx`: 1. Import `PageHeader` from `@/components/shared/PageHeader`. 2. Replace the header block (lines ~24-27): ```tsx

More

Browse more pages and features

``` with: ```tsx

Browse more pages and features

``` 3. The h1 will now get standard responsive sizing classes via PageHeader. This is an intentional normalization. 4. The subtitle `

` stays outside PageHeader as a sibling element.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Run `npx vitest run` to verify all tests pass after Phase 1 refactors.

task.name: Create UserMenu component
task.action: Create `src/components/shared/UserMenu.tsx`: 1. Import dependencies: - `useSession`, `signOut` from `@/lib/auth-client` - `useRouter` from `next/navigation` - `Link` from `next/link` - `DropdownMenu`, `DropdownMenuContent`, `DropdownMenuItem`, `DropdownMenuLabel`, `DropdownMenuSeparator`, `DropdownMenuTrigger` from `@/components/ui/dropdown-menu` - `Settings`, `LogOut`, `ChevronDown` from `lucide-react` 2. Component has no props. Internally calls `useSession()` and `useRouter()`. 3. Define `getUserInitial()` helper (same logic as current Sidebar): - If `session?.user?.name`, return first char uppercased. - Else if `session?.user?.email`, return first char uppercased. - Else return 'U'. 4. Define `handleLogout()` (same as current Sidebar): ``` signOut({ fetchOptions: { onSuccess: () => router.push("/sign-in") } }) ``` 5. If `!session`, return null (do not render anything while session is loading or absent). 6. Render: ```tsx {session.user.name || session.user.email} Settings Log out ``` 7. Use named export: `export { UserMenu }` or `export const UserMenu = ...`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build` to confirm compilation.

task.name: Integrate UserMenu into PageHeader
task.action:
In `src/components/shared/PageHeader.tsx`: 1. Import `UserMenu` from `@/components/shared/UserMenu`. 2. Update the render to always include UserMenu on the right side: ```tsx

{title}

{actions}
``` 3. The right-side div always renders (UserMenu is always present). If there are no actions, it just shows UserMenu.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Remove user section from Sidebar
task.action:
In `src/components/shared/Sidebar.tsx`:
1. Delete the entire user section block (lines ~222-270), which includes: - The `{/* User section */}` comment - The `<div className={...pb-6...}>` wrapper - Both collapsed and expanded user variants (avatar, name, logout button)
2. Delete the `handleLogout` function (lines ~36-43). 3. Delete the `getUserInitial` function (lines ~45-52). 4. Remove these imports that are now unused: - `useSession` and `signOut` from `@/lib/auth-client` (check: useSession was only used for the user section) - `useRouter` from `next/navigation` (check: only used in handleLogout) - `Icons` from `../icons` (check: only Icons.logOut was used; verify no other Icons references remain) 5. Remove the `session` variable declaration from the component body. 6. Remove the `router` variable declaration if useRouter is removed. 7. Keep all other imports and code intact (navigation, quick actions, Sheet/Drawer forms). 8. Verify remaining code compiles -- the Sidebar still needs: `usePathname`, `Link`, `useState`, navigation constants, `useSidebarState`, Tabler icons, Tooltip components, and the create Sheet/Drawer forms.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Verify no references to removed functions or imports remain.

task.name: Create placeholder Settings page
task.action:
Create `src/app/settings/page.tsx`: ```tsx "use client" import { PageHeader } from '@/components/shared/PageHeader' export default function SettingsPage() { return (

Settings page coming soon.

) } ``` This page is NOT added to navigation constants (Sidebar or BottomBar). It is only accessible via the UserMenu dropdown link.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Navigate to /settings in the browser and confirm it renders.

task.name: Create MobileHeroSummary component
task.action:
Create `src/components/dashboard/MobileHeroSummary.tsx`: 1. Import dependencies: - `useMonthlySummary` from `@/hooks/useMonthlySummary` - `TrendingUp`, `TrendingDown`, `Minus` from `lucide-react` - `Skeleton` from `@/components/ui/skeleton` 2. No props. Internally calls `useMonthlySummary()`. 3. Define `formatCurrency` helper (same as MonthlySummaryChart): ``` return `â‚±${value.toLocaleString('en-PH', { minimumFractionDigits: 0, maximumFractionDigits: 0 })}`; ``` 4. Loading state: if `isLoading`, return: ```tsx
``` 5. Error or no data: if `error` or `!summary`, return `null`. 6. Calculate percentage changes (same logic as MonthlySummaryChart): ``` const incomeChange = summary.lastMonth.income > 0 ? ((summary.currentMonth.income - summary.lastMonth.income) / summary.lastMonth.income) * 100 : 0; const expenseChange = summary.lastMonth.expenses > 0 ? ((summary.currentMonth.expenses - summary.lastMonth.expenses) / summary.lastMonth.expenses) * 100 : 0; ``` 7. Define `getChangeIcon(change)` and `getChangeColor(change, isExpense)` helpers (same as MonthlySummaryChart). 8. Render (wrapped in `md:hidden`): ```tsx
{/* Income Card */}
Income {formatCurrency(summary.currentMonth.income)}
{Math.abs(incomeChange).toFixed(0)}% vs last month
{/* Expense Card */}
Expenses {formatCurrency(summary.currentMonth.expenses)}
{Math.abs(expenseChange).toFixed(0)}% vs last month
{/* Net Savings Bar */}
Net savings = 0 ? 'text-text-success' : 'text-text-error'}`}> {formatCurrency(summary.currentMonth.savings)}
``` 9. Use named export.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Add MobileHeroSummary to dashboard page
task.action:
In `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`:
1. Import `MobileHeroSummary` from `@/components/dashboard/MobileHeroSummary`. 2. Add `<MobileHeroSummary />` after `<PageHeader title="Dashboard" />` and before `<NetWorthSection />`: ```tsx <PageHeader title="Dashboard" /> <MobileHeroSummary /> <NetWorthSection /> ``` 3. The dashboard page already has `flex flex-col gap-6` on the container, so the hero summary will have automatic spacing.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Hide MonthlySummaryChart on mobile
task.action:
In `src/components/dashboard/NetWorthInsights.tsx`: 1. Wrap the border separator AND the MonthlySummaryChart together in a `hidden lg:block` container: Current code: ```tsx
``` Replace with: ```tsx
``` 2. IMPORTANT: Both the separator and MonthlySummaryChart must be inside the same hidden wrapper. Do not hide them separately. 3. The `lg:` breakpoint matches the `NetWorthSection` grid breakpoint (`lg:grid-cols-5`), ensuring consistent behavior.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Run `npx vitest run` to verify all tests pass after Phase 3.

task.name: Add back link to Account Detail page
task.action:
In `src/app/accounts/[id]/page.tsx`: 1. Import `Link` from `next/link` (may already be imported -- check first). 2. Import `ArrowLeft` from `lucide-react` (or use `IconArrowLeft` from `@tabler/icons-react` if Tabler icons are already imported in this file). 3. Find the mobile layout block: ```tsx
``` 4. Insert a back link as the FIRST child of the `md:hidden` container, before the existing `flex items-center justify-between` div: ```tsx Accounts ``` 5. Do NOT modify the desktop layout (`hidden md:flex`).
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Add back link to Card Detail page
task.action:
In `src/app/cards/[id]/page.tsx`: 1. Import `Link` from `next/link` (check if already imported). 2. Import `ArrowLeft` from `lucide-react` (or Tabler equivalent if already imported). 3. Find the mobile layout block: ```tsx
``` 4. Insert a back link as the FIRST child: ```tsx Cards ```
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Add back link to Card Group Detail page
task.action:
In `src/app/cards/groups/[groupName]/page.tsx`: 1. Import `Link` from `next/link` (check if already imported). 2. Import `ArrowLeft` from `lucide-react` (or Tabler equivalent if already imported). 3. Find the mobile layout block: ```tsx
``` 4. Insert a back link as the FIRST child: ```tsx Cards ```
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Run `npx vitest run` to verify all tests pass after all phases.
